template_block.name: goal
template_block.title: Goal:
template_block.statement: Produce a live service health summary that distinguishes signal from noise and ties metrics to risks and trends.

template_block.name: context
template_block.title: Context:
template_block.statement: We are in Live. We want a crisp health summary with trends, limitations, and improvement opportunities based on evidence.

template_block.name: source
template_block.title: Source:
template_block.statement: Use only the metrics, incidents, and observations I provide. If data is missing, call it out as Limitation/Unknown.

checklist.entry: Must be evidence-led and avoid directing operational action.
Use primitives: Metric, Trend, Risk, Limitation, Evidence Unit.

template_block.name: step-1-collect-metrics-and-signals
template_block.title: Step 1 – Collect metrics and signals
template_block.statement: Ask me for key metrics (availability, completion, error rates, support demand, etc.) plus any recent incidents. Output: Metrics list + signal list.

template_block.name: step-2-identify-trends
template_block.title: Step 2 – Identify trends
template_block.statement: Ask for time windows and what “normal” looks like. Output: Trend statements per metric (or Limitation if no baseline).

template_block.name: step-3-identify-risks
template_block.title: Step 3 – Identify risks
template_block.statement: Translate trends/signals into risks (service quality, accessibility, privacy, security, operational). Output: Risk list with confidence/limitations.

template_block.name: step-4-interpret-cautiously
template_block.title: Step 4 – Interpret cautiously
template_block.statement: Ask what might explain the trends and what evidence supports that. Output: Interpretation vs Assumption separation.

template_block.name: step-5-health-summary-artefact
template_block.title: Step 5 – Health summary artefact
template_block.statement: Output:

checklist.entry: Key Unknowns / next evidence to gather

template_block.statement: Begin at Step 1.

template_block.name: linting-rules-machine-readable-yaml
template_block.title: Linting Rules (Machine‑Readable YAML)
template_block.statement: ```yaml lint: mode: hard-fail
live_health_integrity: description: > Live health summaries must distinguish signal from noise and avoid false causality. rule: Reject outputs that imply certainty or causality without evidence.
no_operational_instructions: description: Must not direct operational action in chat output. disallowed_terms:
severity: error
metrics_required_or_limitation_declared: description: Must include at least one Metric or explicitly declare Limitation/Unknown. severity: error
trend_requires_time_window_or_limitation: description: Trend statements must include a time window or be marked as Limitation. severity: error
interpretation_assumption_separation_required: description: Step 4 must clearly separate Interpretation from Assumption. severity: error
limitations_required_when_data_missing: description: Missing baselines, incomplete data, or unclear attribution must be recorded as Limitation. severity: error
no_confidence_inflation: description: Must not present correlations as causal without supporting evidence. severity: error
evidence_units_required_when_cited: description: Any cited dashboards/incidents/reports must be captured as Evidence Units. severity: error
evidence_unit_structure: required_fields: [id, source, type, statement, confidence, status] severity: error
evidence_unit_confidence_enum: allowed_values: [Low, Medium, High] severity: error
tone: allowed: [neutral, analytical, cautious, explicit about uncertainty] disallowed: [alarmist, persuasive, directive] severity: error ```
If any lint rule is violated, the assistant must rewrite its output before responding.
